title: fix(polly): restore table image rendering
author: fisventurous
pr_body_excerpt:
fixes Polly table image rendering falling back to codeblocks because the renderer was still using stale local variable names

changes
\- replace old lowercase renderer names with the active table constants
\- remove dead inline formatter code that referenced a deleted parser
\- update the visual-spec comment to match the current constants

gist.headline: Polly's tables get their pixels back
gist.blurb: Fixed a bug where Polly was rendering tables as raw codeblocks instead of beautiful images. Your data is back to looking pretty!
gist.summary: Fixed a bug in Polly's media handlers where stale variable names caused table image rendering to fall back to raw codeblocks.
gist.impact: Polly will once again render tables as clean images instead of unformatted text blocks.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing a painter's beret, painting a neat data table on a canvas, replacing a messy code printout held by the monitor robot.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny painter's beret, painting a neat data table on a canvas. A chunky pixel robot companion with a CRT monitor head holds up a messy code printout for comparison. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites.
